sentence_reviews.text: Structured-decoding runtimes compile grammars or schemas into efficient token filters integrated with a model server’s decoding loop.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Structured-output survey supports grammar/schema-constrained generation and token-level constraints; efficient model-server runtime integration is more specific than the citation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Add a runtime implementation source for model-server integration and efficiency.

sentence_reviews.text: They make reliable structured generation practical at serving scale instead of relying only on slow application-side retries.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Structured-output survey supports reliability motivation; DDIA supports serving-scale concerns, but the retry comparison is not directly sourced.
sentence_reviews.notes: Plausible but should be backed by runtime benchmark or implementation evidence.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Structured-output survey supports schema-constrained output generation and token constraints.
sentence_reviews.notes: Concept is supported, though exact implementation phrase is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Structured-output survey is relevant to typed outputs, but tool-call serving is not directly established in packet evidence.
sentence_reviews.notes: Needs a tool-calling or structured serving source.

sentence_reviews.text: Grammar compilation, tokenizer interaction, and per-request constraints can reduce throughput or complicate batching.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sources support constrained decoding and serving-scale concerns generally; throughput/batching impact is not directly evidenced.
sentence_reviews.notes: Add benchmark/runtime evidence for performance claims.

sentence_reviews.text: Structured-decoding runtimes turns Grammar- and schema-constrained decoding into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
sentence_reviews.notes: Core dependency is well supported conceptually.

sentence_reviews.text: Structured-decoding runtimes turns Model servers & inference runtimes into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sources support constrained generation and data-system operations broadly; model serving linkage is synthesized.
sentence_reviews.notes: Core dependency is plausible; needs serving-runtime citation for exact support.

sentence_reviews.text: Structured-decoding runtimes turns Graph compilers, runtimes & operator fusion into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The structured-output source supports constrained decoding, not graph compilers or operator fusion.
sentence_reviews.notes: Compiler/runtime dependency should be narrowed or sourced.

edge_reviews.notes: Grammar/schema-constrained decoding is central to the runtime.
Serving integration is plausible for runtime deployment, but direct evidence is missing.
Compilation of grammars may justify a runtime dependency, but graph compiler/operator-fusion wording is too broad.
